name: ScaNN (Scalable Nearest Neighbors by Google)

performance.notes: State-of-the-art ANN performance; Google's internal search backend

agent_readiness.notes: Google's approximate nearest neighbor library. import scann. searcher = scann.scann_ops_pybind.builder(db, 10, 'dot_product').tree(num_leaves=2000).score_ah(2).reorder(100).build(). searcher.search_batched(queries). Asymmetric hashing for quantization. Tree-AH (tree with asymmetric hashing) for billion-scale. State-of-the-art ANN-benchmarks performance. Linux-only (no macOS pip install). TensorFlow dependency. Powers Google's internal similarity search.

gotchas: Linux-only via pip (macOS requires source compilation)
Builder API is verbose and less intuitive than faiss/hnswlib
best_when: When state-of-the-art ANN-benchmarks recall/QPS ratio needed
avoid_when: macOS development (use faiss or hnswlib instead)
Dynamic index updates needed (faiss IVFPQ or hnswlib better)
When TensorFlow dependency is a blocker
summary: ScaNN is Google's approximate nearest neighbor library achieving top ANN-benchmarks performance via asymmetric hashing and tree-AH indexing. searcher = scann.scann_ops_pybind.builder(db, 10, 'dot_product').tree().score_ah().build(). Billion-scale capable. Linux-only via pip. TensorFlow dependency. Powers Google's internal similarity search. Use faiss or hnswlib for cross-platform development.
